assertions.title: Rust analyze command succeeds on a healthy replay

steps.action: Run `source .venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH=src python -m rlcoach.cli analyze Replay_files/testing_replay.replay --adapter rust --out /tmp/rlcoach-ut-analyze-success --pretty` from the repo root
steps.observed: Command exited with code 0 and printed `/tmp/rlcoach-ut-analyze-success/testing_replay.json`; the file exists at the assigned output path

assertions.title: Analyze output is schema-valid success payload

steps.action: Load the generated JSON and validate it against `schemas/replay_report.schema.json` with the environment's `jsonschema` package
steps.expected: The report matches the success branch of the schema and does not validate as an error payload
steps.observed: Validation succeeded with `valid: true`; the JSON contains the required success keys (`replay_id`, `schema_version`, `metadata`, `quality`, `teams`, `players`, `events`, `analysis`) and no `error` object

assertions.title: Analyze output preserves parser diagnostics and provenance

steps.action: Inspect `quality.parser` and nested diagnostics fields in the generated JSON
steps.expected: Rust parser health/provenance is explicit, including parser status, frames emitted, usable parse details, and attempted backend information
steps.observed: `quality.parser` includes `name: rust`, `version: 0.1.0`, `parsed_header: true`, `parsed_network_data: true`, `crc_checked: true`, `network_diagnostics.status: ok`, `network_diagnostics.frames_emitted: 14661`, `attempted_backends: ["boxcars"]`, and scorecard coverage/provenance fields including `usable_network_parse: true`, `player_identity_coverage: 1.0`, and `network_frame_count: 14661`

assertions.title: Analyze output exposes expanded header metadata

steps.action: Inspect the report `metadata` block for expanded replay/header fields
steps.expected: Concrete header metadata fields are present and populated when available, including match identity and replay metadata fields

summary: Validated 4 analyze success-path assertions through the local CLI surface. The Rust analyze command completed successfully on `Replay_files/testing_replay.replay`, produced `/tmp/rlcoach-ut-analyze-success/testing_replay.json`, the output validated against `schemas/replay_report.schema.json` as a success payload, and the report preserved explicit parser diagnostics/provenance plus expanded header metadata fields required by the mission.
